codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal fix that correctly resolves the Clerk SignInButton multiple-children console error. The worker extracted the SignInButton+button usage from the server component page.tsx into a dedicated 'use client' component (SignInPromptButton), ensuring React.Children.only sees exactly one child element within the client boundary. The redirectUrl prop is properly threaded through, mode='modal' and forceRedirectUrl are preserved, and the button styling is identical to the original. Test mocks were correctly updated to mock the new component path instead of @clerk/nextjs directly. All 22 page tests pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly, and ESLint reports no issues.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The 'use client' extraction pattern for Clerk interactive components (SignInButton, UserButton) used within server component pages is not documented in AGENTS.md. This pattern — wrapping Clerk client components in a dedicated 'use client' wrapper to avoid server/client boundary serialization issues — is now established by this fix and should be the standard approach when using Clerk interactive components in server component contexts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/extension/check/sign-in-prompt-button.tsx created as 'use client' wrapper. src/app/page.tsx:48 still uses the old pattern (SignInButton directly in what appears to be a server component context) — the worker flagged this as a discovered issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from TDD (write tests first) because this was a structural refactoring that didn't change behavior — existing tests covered the functionality. The deviation was justified and the skill's TDD step could note that for refactoring/fix features that preserve behavior, updating existing tests post-implementation is acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Updated existing test mocks after implementation since this was a refactoring fix (extracting to client component) rather than new behavior'

summary: PASS. The fix correctly resolves the Clerk '@clerk/react: You've passed multiple children components to <SignInButton/>' console error by extracting SignInButton usage into a dedicated 'use client' component (SignInPromptButton). The implementation is minimal (13-line new file), preserves all original behavior and styling, and tests were properly updated. No blocking issues found.
